Clamp negative to 0 (hint formula): Output only. For single inputs use input neg→0.
Clamp negative to 0 (hint source): Output only (does not change the source state).
Clamp negative to 0 (tooltip): Clamps only the item result (output). If you want to clamp just one source/input, enable neg→0 on that input.
Configure values via the Master/Detail e: Configure values via the Master/Detail editor.
Formula syntax: soc < 10, battery > 80 &: Formula syntax: soc < 10, battery > 80 && surplus > 0, true (for default/fallback)
Insert building blocks on the left. The: Insert building blocks on the left. The editor uses current (unsaved) inputs.
Master/detail editor for sensors. Shows: Master/detail editor for sensors. Shows enabled status live in the list.
Name: Name
Pick a state id and insert jp(\"id\", \": Pick a state id and insert jp("id", "$.value")
Pick a state id and insert s(\"id\"): Pick a state id and insert s("id")
Pick a state id and insert v(\"id\"): Pick a state id and insert v("id")
Preview not supported for state function: Preview not supported for state functions
Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom; first: Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom; first matching rule wins.
SOLECTRUS Quick Start (InfluxDB):
SOLECTRUS Quick Start
1. Create an InfluxDB 2.x API token with write access to your bucket.
2. Enter URL, organization, bucket and token on the left and save.
3. Open the Sensors tab and map your ioBroker states.
4. Enable the adapter and check info.connection and the buffer states.
Select a forecast on the left or add a n: Select a forecast on the left or add a new one.
Select a sensor on the left or add a new: Select a sensor on the left or add a new one.
Select an item on the left or add a new: Select an item on the left or add a new one.
Tip: You can still edit the formula as p: Tip: You can still edit the formula as plain text anytime.
aliveTimeoutHint: If the last timestamp of the source state exceeds the timeout, a warning is logged. Note that the timeout must be greater than the update interval of the source adapters. Default: 60 min.
dsValuesHint: Configure computed values via the Master/Detail editor. Each value can mirror a source state or compute a formula from multiple inputs.
e.g. soc < 10 or true for default: e.g. soc < 10 or true for default
fn.clamp: Clamps a value between min and max. Example: clamp(value, 0, 100)
fn.max: Returns the larger of two values. Example: max(5, 10) = 10
fn.min: Returns the smaller of two values. Example: min(5, 10) = 5
forecastEntryHint: The adapter subscribes to the JSON source state and writes each array entry to InfluxDB using the timestamp from the JSON data. Multiple entries can point to the same source but extract different value fields (e.g. y, clearsky, temp).
forecastHint: Configure forecast sources from pvforecast or similar adapters. The adapter subscribes to a JSON state, parses the array, and writes each entry to InfluxDB with the timestamp from the JSON data. Existing points are automatically updated when forecast data changes.
iframeCertHint: Page not loading? SOLECTRUS blocks iframe embedding by default – add FRAME_ANCESTORS=http://your-iobroker-host:8081 to the SOLECTRUS .env file and restart the service.
iframeHint: Enter the direct URL of your SOLECTRUS dashboard (e.g. http://solectrus:3000). Do not use the ioBroker Web Adapter proxy URL – it adds a path prefix that causes 404 errors for SOLECTRUS asset requests. Leave empty to hide the iFrame tab. SOLECTRUS blocks iframe embedding by default: add FRAME_ANCESTORS=http://your-iobroker-host:8081 to the SOLECTRUS .env file and restart the service. If your admin panel runs on HTTPS but SOLECTRUS uses HTTP, use a dedicated reverse proxy (e.g. nginx or Caddy) forwarding to SOLECTRUS at the root path.
iframeHttpHint: Note: The iframe is only visible when accessing the ioBroker admin via HTTP (not HTTPS).
jsonAutoDetectInfo:
y → inverter_forecast:power (Forecast)
clearsky → inverter_forecast_clearsky:power (Clearsky)
temp → outdoor_forecast:temperature (Temperature)

Fields not present in the JSON are skipped automatically.
jsonSensorDetailHint: The source state must contain a JSON array (e.g. from pvforecast). Each array entry is parsed and written directly to InfluxDB with the original timestamp. In automatic mode, the adapter detects known fields (y, clearsky, temp) and writes them to the correct measurements. For custom types, specify the JSON key for timestamp and value.
jsonSensorHint: Forecast/weather data can be configured as JSON sensors in the Sensors tab (datatype: JSON Array).
maxValueHint: Per-sensor plausibility limit. If exceeded, the last valid value is sent instead and a warning is logged. Default: 0 (disabled).
negativeValuesHint: Note: SOLECTRUS does not accept negative values. If a sensor delivers negative values, a warning will be logged once after adapter start. Check your source states or use the Data-SOLECTRUS formula engine with the "Clamp negative to 0" option.
nonExpertExpertHint: The mapping can be customized in Expert Mode.
nonExpertJsonInfo:
The adapter reads a JSON array from the source state (e.g. from pvforecast) and writes the forecast data with the original timestamp to InfluxDB.

%MAPPING%

Fields not present in the JSON are skipped automatically.
nonExpertMonitoringDisabled:
Monitoring disabled.
Configurable in Expert Mode.
nonExpertMonitoringInfo:
Monitoring active: timeout %TIMEOUTSTR%.
Configurable in Expert Mode.
nonExpertMonitoringInfoFull:
Monitoring active: values up to %MAXWSTR% · timeout %TIMEOUTSTR%.
Configurable in Expert Mode.
nonExpertSensorInfo: The adapter writes the measured value as %TYPE% into %MF%.
notifyHint: Enable notifications and select the providers you want to use. Available adapter instances are automatically read and offered in dropdown lists.
notifyOnConnectionFailLabel: Notify on InfluxDB connection failure / restore
notifyOnMaxValueExceededLabel: Notify when sensor value exceeds maximum
op.!: Logical NOT: Inverts a boolean value. Example: !condition
op.!=: Not equal: Tests for inequality. Example: a != 0
op.&&: Logical AND: Both conditions must be true. Example: a > 0 && b > 0
op.(): Parentheses: Controls calculation order. Example: (a + b) * c
op.*: Multiplication: Multiplies two values. Example: a * b
op.+: Addition: Adds two values. Example: a + b
op.-: Subtraction: Subtracts one value from another. Example: a - b
op./: Division: Divides one value by another. Example: a / b
op.<: Less than: Compares two values. Example: a < 100
op.<=: Less or equal: Compares two values. Example: a <= 100
op.==: Equal: Tests for equality. Example: a == 5
op.>: Greater than: Compares two values. Example: a > 0
op.>=: Greater or equal: Compares two values. Example: a >= 10
op.?:: Ternary operator: If-Then-Else shorthand. Example: condition ? valueIfTrue : valueIfFalse
op.||: Logical OR: At least one condition must be true. Example: a > 10 || b > 10
